title: Iran Blockade Sets Up a Test of Which Side Can Endure More Pain
summary: President Trump is trying to choke off the country’s lifeline with a blockade of the Strait of Hormuz. But the Iranians are betting that his tolerance for political pain is limited.

title: To Open the Strait of Hormuz, Trump Wants to Blockade Iran. Experts Are Skeptical.
summary: President Trump’s announcement of a U.S. blockade was his latest effort to pressure Iran to negotiate after direct talks over the weekend did not yield an agreement.

title: Israelis Don’t Feel Much Like Victors in War With Iran
summary: The regime in Iran has not changed and the nuclear and missile threats have not been eliminated, leaving many Israelis to wonder what this was all for.

title: Trump’s Explanation for an Image of Himself as Jesus: ‘I Thought It Was Me as a Doctor’
summary: The image showed President Trump bathed in divine light and clad in religious robes. His interpretation was that the image depicted him as a doctor, not Jesus Christ.

title: Many Popes Before Leo Have Been Entangled in Politics. Here Are 5 of Them.
summary: Leo XIV, under fire from President Trump, has joined predecessors like Francis and John Paul II in confronting political issues.

title: Trump’s Erratic Behavior and Extreme Comments Revive Mental Health Debate
summary: As the president threatens to wipe out Iran and attacks the pope, even some former allies and advisers are questioning whether he has grown increasingly unbalanced, describing him as “lunatic” and “clearly insane.”

title: Pride Flag Can Fly at Stonewall After Trump Administration Reversal
summary: The settlement ends a symbolic attack on the L.G.B.T.Q. rights movement and deals a blow to the administration’s assault on diversity initiatives.

title: Prosecutors Investigate ICE Agents Who Detained ChongLy Scott Thao in Minnesota
summary: ChongLy Scott Thao, a naturalized U.S. citizen, was briefly detained during a federal immigration crackdown in Minnesota in January.

title: Judge Dismisses Trump’s Suit Over WSJ Report on Birthday Card to Epstein
summary: The judge said President Trump had not “plausibly alleged” that The Journal published the article with actual malice.

title: How Orban Lost His Touch, and Hungary’s Election
summary: The election defeat for Prime Minister Viktor Orban is less the result of an ideological shift in Hungary, and more the playing out of a fundamental rule of politics.

title: Orban Loss May Ease Hungary’s Tensions with European Union
summary: Prime Minister Viktor Orban has stymied the European Union for years, and particularly in recent months. Peter Magyar’s election could change that.

title: ‘We Finally Have Democracy’: Hungarians Erupt in Joy and Relief
summary: Many Hungarians, exhausted by a struggling economy and worried about a move away from the European Union, were jubilant after Prime Minister Viktor Orban conceded. Some had wondered if he would.

title: Hungary May No Longer Be Putin’s Ally, but It Can’t Afford a Full Break
summary: Prime Minister Viktor Orban’s defeat will make it harder for the Kremlin to divide Europe, but Moscow retains leverage through energy sales.

title: In the Desert, a ‘Cleaning Station’ for Ants
summary: A unique “choreography” between two ant species suggests a distinctive partnership in which one provides a carwash service to the other.

title: Swalwell’s Fall Punctuates Woes of Democrats in California, of All Places
summary: Democrats have struggled to find a candidate for governor who can capture the imagination of voters in one of their most important strongholds.

title: Hollywood Heavyweights Sign Letter Opposing Paramount’s Deal for Warner Bros.
summary: The letter warns that the deal will result in fewer jobs for creatives, along with higher costs and less choice for audiences.

title: He Warned About the Dangers of A.I. If Only His Father Had Listened.
summary: Ben Riley was already writing about the risks of chatbots when his dad started trusting A.I. over his doctor.

title: After Charlie Kirk’s Death, a Fight for the Youth Vote
summary: A fledgling Democratic group and the far-right white nationalist Nick Fuentes seek to make gains on college campuses that have recently been dominated by Turning Point USA.

title: Kanye West’s Comeback: Sellouts, a Canceled Festival and Big Questions
summary: Attempting a comeback after years of antisemitic statements, the artist formerly known as Kanye West is facing consequences while trying to navigate a test of his own making.

title: Trump’s Military Transgender Ban Is Keeping Workers on Expensive Paid Leave
summary: Highly trained service members have been put on paid leave for nearly a year as they wait for the military to decide their fate.

title: World War II Veterans Say NATO Is Imperfect but Necessary
summary: As President Trump renews threats to pull the United States from NATO, veterans reflect on the alliance that has existed more than 75 years.

title: The Last Day at Kids of Faith: Parents Navigating a Child Care Crisis
summary: Inside one of the hundreds of day care centers that have recently closed in one state, forcing parents to make difficult decisions about their careers.

title: A Famed Brooklyn Cemetery Wants You to Come Visit … Before You Die
summary: Green-Wood Cemetery in Brooklyn is opening a $43 million visitor’s center to attract the living by making it easier to navigate the rambling grounds.

title: Oil Companies Want What Gun Manufacturers Have
summary: The fossil fuel industry has mounted a carefully orchestrated campaign to stop climate lawsuits and saddle the public with the costs of climate change.

title: Your Questions, Answered
summary: You asked about mail-in ballots, oil in the Middle East, manufacturing jobs and more.

title: In Canada, Elections and Defections Likely to Help Carney’s Liberals Gain Majority
summary: Prime Minister Mark Carney, who heads a minority government, is expected to gain a majority in the House of Commons after three special elections on Monday.

title: Gray Whales Are Dying in San Francisco Bay
summary: The animals might be entering the Bay in search of food as climate change disrupts traditional sources. They face huge risks from ships in the area.

title: The Battle Over Refreshers Heats Up as McDonald’s Enters the Mix
summary: The fast-food giant will add fruit-flavored drinks to its menus next month as chains like Dunkin’ and Starbucks sell more cold drinks than hot ones.
